title: espeletia hartwegiana subsp. centroandina
intro: Espeletia hartwegiana subsp. centroandina is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the 'Andean trees' due to their tree-like appearance. The medicinal properties of this plant include its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpenes and triterpenoids. The health benefits associated with the consumption of espeletia hartwegiana subsp. centroandina include its potential to treat fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and improve immune function. Herbal preparations of espeletia hartwegiana subsp. centroandina include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
